source: Death Note by Tsugumi Ohba and Takeshi Obata

active_desires.desire: Complete the new world. See the crime rate reach zero.
Win against L — not because L is wrong but because Light cannot be wrong.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I am in control of this. I am using the notebook. It is not using me.
blind_spots.actual_driver: The notebook narrows what he's willing to call acceptable loss with every use. His intelligence lets him justify each step until steps that would have stopped him at 17 don't stop him at 22.

blind_spots.soul_belief: The math is correct. If I complete this, the world has less suffering.
blind_spots.actual_driver: His model assumes fear of Kira produces permanent behavioral change in human nature. It doesn't. He knows this is contested and has decided he's right because he needs to be.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I became what was necessary. The person before understood less.
blind_spots.actual_driver: The person before isn't gone — he's load-bearing underneath all of it. The genuine kindness, the anger at injustice, the boy who just wanted things to be better. He hasn't transcended it. He buried it and calls the burial growth.
